beginner_guide_desc: Not sure how to choose your first cigar? Let us recommend the best beginner-friendly cigar series. From mild to medium, from mini to standard size, find your perfect first cigar.

bs_page_title: A Century of Cuban Cigar Heritage | ShangJia
bs_page_description: From the fertile soils of Vuelta Abajo to the cigar workshops of Havana, explore 500 years of Cuban cigar history, traditional craftsmanship and 27 legendary brands.
bs_hero_title: A Century of Cuban Cigar Heritage
bs_hero_desc: From the moment Columbus set foot on the New World, Cuban tobacco began its legendary journey spanning over five centuries. Every Cuban cigar is a perfect union of the earth's essence and the artisan's soul.
bs_history_desc: From a single tobacco leaf to a legend, the story of Cuban cigars spans five centuries
bs_history_1_desc: Columbus's crew first witnessed the indigenous Taino people smoking rolled tobacco leaves in Cuba — the earliest European record of tobacco.
bs_history_2_desc: Spanish colonists began large-scale tobacco cultivation in Cuba. The island's unique red soil and temperate climate proved ideal for growing premium tobacco leaves.
bs_history_3_desc: Legendary brands including Partagas (1845), Hoyo de Monterrey (1865), and Romeo y Julieta (1875) were founded, ushering in the golden age of Cuban cigars.
bs_history_4_desc: The Cuban government began implementing denomination of origin protection for tobacco regions, ensuring the quality and authenticity of 'Habanos'. Vuelta Abajo was officially recognized as the finest tobacco region.
bs_history_5_desc: The Cohiba brand was born, initially serving exclusively as the private cigar supply for Cuban leaders. Its unique triple fermentation process became the pinnacle of Cuban cigar craftsmanship.
bs_history_6_desc: Cuba established Habanos S.A. as the exclusive global distributor of Cuban cigars, unifying the production and sales management of all Cuban cigar brands.
bs_history_7_desc: Cuban cigars continue the tradition of entirely handmade production while constantly introducing limited and special editions, perfectly blending centuries of craftsmanship with modern taste. The annual Habanos Festival has become a pilgrimage for cigar enthusiasts worldwide.
bs_terroir_desc: Cuba's tobacco regions are like the vineyards of Burgundy — every inch of land has its own unique character. It is these irreplicable terroir conditions that give Cuban cigars their inimitable soul.
bs_terroir_image_caption: Vuelta Abajo — The Holy Land of World Tobacco
bs_terroir_r1_desc: Located in Cuba's westernmost Pinar del Río province, universally recognized as the world's finest tobacco region. Its unique red soil, stable temperatures and ideal humidity produce the most complex and refined tobacco leaves.
bs_terroir_r2_desc: Adjacent to Vuelta Abajo, this region produces tobacco leaves primarily used as filler and binder, with equally outstanding quality.
bs_terroir_r3_desc: South of Havana, renowned for growing premium wrapper leaves. The shade-growing (Tapado) technique reaches its peak here.
bs_terroir_r4_desc: Located in eastern Cuba, a historic tobacco region producing distinctively flavored leaves that provide unique ingredients for multiple brands.
bs_terroir_r5_desc: Cuba's easternmost region. Though smaller in output, its tobacco carries unique terroir characteristics, often used in special edition cigar blends.
bs_craft_desc: A Cuban cigar undergoes over 200 processes from planting to finished product, taking 2-5 years. Every step is the ultimate test of patience and skill.
bs_craft_image_caption: Torcedor — Guardian of Cuban Cigar Making
bs_craft_s1_desc: Tobacco planting begins each October, with harvest after approximately 120 days of careful tending. Cuba's Vegueros (tobacco farmers) pass down cultivation techniques through generations, from seed selection and nursery to shade control.
bs_craft_s2_desc: Harvested leaves are naturally air-cured for 45-60 days in a Casa de Tabaco (curing barn), then undergo multiple rounds of fermentation. Cohiba's exclusive third fermentation round produces its distinctively smooth and refined flavor.
bs_craft_s3_desc: Experienced sorters strictly classify leaves by color, texture and size. Premium leaves are aged for months or even years in temperature and humidity-controlled warehouses, developing richer flavors.
bs_craft_s4_desc: The Torcedor (cigar roller) uses a Chaveta (half-moon knife) and a wooden board to perfectly combine selected filler, binder and wrapper. A skilled roller can complete only 60-150 cigars per day, depending on the format.
bs_craft_s5_desc: Every cigar must pass rigorous quality inspection, including tests for appearance, weight, firmness and draw resistance. Non-conforming products are ruthlessly rejected, ensuring every cigar represents Cuba's highest standards.
bs_craft_s6_desc: Finished cigars rest in the Escaparate (aging room) for weeks to months, allowing the flavors of filler, binder and wrapper to fully marry. Finally, the Escogedora (color sorter) arranges them by wrapper shade for boxing.
bs_brands_desc: 27 Cuban cigar brands, each with its own unique style and soul
bs_brand_cohiba_desc: The king of Cuban cigars, originally reserved exclusively for Cuban leaders. Famous for its unique triple fermentation process, the Behike series is the holy grail of the cigar world.
bs_brand_montecristo_desc: The world's best-selling Cuban cigar brand, winning countless enthusiasts with its balanced flavor and consistent quality. The No.2 is the world's most famous torpedo cigar.
bs_brand_partagas_desc: One of Cuba's oldest cigar brands, renowned for its bold, full-bodied flavor. The Serie D No.4 is one of the most popular Robusto formats.
bs_brand_romeo_desc: Named after Shakespeare's famous play, it was always Winston Churchill's favorite. The Churchill format was named in his honor, and the Wide Churchill is the most successful recent release.
bs_brand_hoyo_desc: An elegantly smooth brand, the Epicure series is beloved by both beginners and seasoned connoisseurs alike. The No.2 is hailed as the perfect Robusto.
bs_brand_bolivar_desc: Named after South American liberator Simón Bolívar, one of Cuba's most full-bodied brands. The Belicosos Finos is its most iconic format.
bs_brand_trinidad_desc: Once used as diplomatic gifts alongside Cohiba, only released to the public in 1998. Known for its elegant, refined flavor and exquisite appearance.
bs_brand_upmann_desc: A legendary brand founded by a German banker, known for its refined, elegant mild-to-medium flavor. The Magnum 46 and 50 series are the most sought-after recent formats.
bs_brand_punch_desc: Named after the British humor magazine, one of Cuba's oldest brands. The Punch Punch is its signature format.
bs_cta_desc: Now that you've discovered the century-old heritage of Cuban cigar making, it's time to experience this gift from the Caribbean firsthand. From the classic Montecristo No.2 to the rare Cohiba Behike, find your perfect cigar at ShangJia.
